title: Evangelion and Dantes The Divine Comedy
display_title: Evangelion and Dantes The Divine Comedy
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion and Dante's "The Divine Comedy"             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Tue Jul 08, 2008 4:50 pm I was just watching Episodes 1 and 2 today, and I think I may have caught a reference to The Divine Comedy by Dante Alighieri. In this post, I'm just going from what I remember reading about Dante's writings. (I KNEW I should have kept those textbooks...they'd've come in handy right now.) By the way, I'm not sure if anyone else picked up on this. I didn't see it anywhere in the Wiki. I warn you, however, this may be a load of old cobblers. Feel free to correct me wherever I need correcting. You know how the protagonist must descend through the circles of Hell? Well, the Devil exists in the lowest circle--the coldest of them all, according to Dante. (Hell, in those days, was a cold and lonely place.) Anyway, the Devil himself is described as being a huge, winged beast, encased in ice up to his chest. (It's this part I want to focus on.) Flash-forward to Evangelion . Remember how Ritsuko et al are always going on about "Unit 00/01/02 in cryo-stasis"? Well, in Episode 2 we get a view of Unit 00 encased nearly up to her chest in ice (dura-bakelite), suspended in mid-punch...attacking the traitor, just as Dante's Devil punishes the three worst betrayers (Judas Iscariot being one of them). Of course, this is to say nothing about the Geofront itself, and how Central Dogma may indeed be the last circle of Hell...

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Jul 08, 2008 5:09 pm A direct reference in episode 24 HYUGA: A developing AT Field has been detected in Central Dogma! Misato: Is that Unit 02?! HYUGA: No, its pattern is blue! There's no doubt about it! It's an Angel! Misato: What did you say?! An Angel? That boy? MAN (OFF): Target has passed through Level 4 and is still descending! AOBA: It's no good! We can't cut the power to the linear carriage! MAN (OFF): Target has passed through Level 5! FUYUTSUKI: Emergency lockdown! Close all partitions in Central Dogma! Buy us some time, no matter how little. ANNOUNCEMENT: Emergency lockdown, all Malebolge levels being closed. All personnel, evacuate. All personnel, evacuate. FUYUTSUKI: I never imagined Seele would send one directly to us. IKARI: The old men intend to step up the schedule by forcing our hand. ***** MAN 02: Humans forget their foolishness and repeat their mistakes. MAN A: If humans do not redeem themselves willingly, they will not change. MAN B: We will not rely on the powers of Adam or the Angels. KEEL: Our only choice is to make changes with our own hands for the future. I will be praying that Unit 01 will accomplish its task. ***** AOBA: Unit 02 is breaking through the armored partitions! HYUGA: Target has passed through Cocytus 2!

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Tue Jul 08, 2008 5:23 pm Had a funny feeling Malebolge/Cocytus meant something, but then we never discussed the names of the Levels in class! It never came up in the textbooks either. Still, at least I caught onto the visual motif of Unit 00 being encased in ice. As for the cosmology thing, well...let's just say it's an interesting project for Gendo to decorate his floor with--that is, if he's got enough SPACE for it.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Jul 08, 2008 5:31 pm Sharaz Destler wrote: t never came up in the textbooks either.

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Tue Jul 08, 2008 9:50 pm Ah, not quite what I was implying, there, Mr. Tines! In our humanities class, we had 2 textbooks (I go to community college): one was a history book, the other was "selected readings". I was fascinated by the Inferno section of it (read: edited parts); it is simply that the names of each Circle were not, to the best of my memory, mentioned . However, it's the point that counts, and the description of the Devil (and, by extension, of Hell itself) is what really stuck out at me. However, I will make a mental note to read the whole thing. I thank you for the advice. :)

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Jul 08, 2008 10:11 pm Nice catch! I remember starting The Divine Comedy back in High School. Been thinking about picking it up again and reading through all of it...

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Wed Jul 09, 2008 4:48 pm aren't bolges and malebolges the sub-levels of the rings of hell? Cocytus is the frozen lake of hell that Satan and other baddies are frozen in. And remember the people that aren't so lucky to be in Satan's mouths are also frozen inside the lake, in varying depths based on if they were a traitor to family, to country, to guests, or to benefactors (am i missing anything?). like there were guys up to their waists frozen, and others up to their neck. Betraying one's benefactor grants you a eternity completely under the ice, in a painfully impossible position... Anyways I remember this part of EoE when selected levels of Central Dogma was pumped full of Bakelite to try and halt the JSSDF from entering the bridge room. Then was Anno also trying to say that the people that were still within those levels, bodies or alive, were traitors to their benefactors? And if that is true, would that make the benefactor SEELE or Lilith?

[]:             Quote Post by Noephon » Wed Jul 09, 2008 11:30 pm TheAyanamiOtaku wrote: aren't bolges and malebolges the sub-levels of the rings of hell?

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Thu Jul 10, 2008 9:05 am And I thought I was well-read...
